sources.title: UBS Reiterates Neutral Rating on Blue Owl Capital (OWL)
sources.excerpt: Blue Owl Capital Inc. (NYSE:[OWL](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/OWL)) is one of the [**10 Best Stocks to Buy Under $10**](https://www.insidermonkey.com/blog/10-best-stocks-to-buy- under-10-1782697/). On June 11, UBS reaffirmed its Neutral rating on Blue Owl Capital Inc. (NYSE:OWL) with a price target of $9.50 on the stock. The research firm's bull-bear analysis suggests that while some upside potential exists for Blue Owl Capital Inc. (NYSE:OWL), the probability does not favor it because of the uncertainty around the reacceleration of private credit growth and business development companies. UBS Reiterates Neutral Rating on Blue Owl Capital (OWL) According to UBS, the shares are expected to remain range-bound. The firm believes that bear points continue to outnumber and outweigh bull points. UBS said that turning constructive on Blue Owl Capital Inc. (NYSE:OWL) would require confidence in a sustainable low-to-mid-teens fee-related earnings compound annual growth rate. The firm sees this as a challenge that could be difficult for the company to clear through 2028. After a decline from its peak, the stock trades at around 9.9 times UBS's 2027 distributable EPS estimate and 9.4 times market estimates. The research firm noted that in recent weeks, pressure on Blue Owl Capital Inc. (NYSE:OWL) has eased a little and there has been a modest uptick in interest, though with low conviction. Blue Owl Capital Inc. (NYSE:OWL) is a leading alternative asset management company that operates through three business lines, namely credit, real assets, and GP Strategic Capital. It provides private capital solutions to businesses and offers differentiated alternative investment opportunities to institutional investors, individual investors, and insurance companies. While we acknowledge th

analysis:
- **External commentary:** UBS reiterated a Neutral rating on OWL with a $9.50 price target, stating that bear points still outnumber bull points and that a sustainable low-to-mid-teens fee-related earnings CAGR through 2028 is a difficult challenge [3]. Separately, unconfirmed market chatter indicates Blue Owl is in advanced talks to buy a 5–10% minority stake in the NBA’s Cleveland Cavaliers [1][2]; the full details are behind a paywall and the company’s response is not publicly available in the excerpts.

- **Why it can work:** The stock trades at a forward P/E of 9.58 and a PEG of 0.14, suggesting low valuation relative to expected earnings growth. Short interest is elevated at 20.6% of float, which could fuel a squeeze if positive catalysts emerge. The potential Cavaliers stake, if confirmed and accretive, could signal new alternative investment verticals and broaden OWL’s asset base.

- **What can break it:** UBS explicitly cites uncertainty around reacceleration of private credit growth and BDC performance as key headwinds [3]. The stock is down 59.3% from its 52-week high and has underperformed its sector by 20.9% in the past month. The trailing P/E of 78.5 implies recent earnings are depressed, and the negative sentiment score (-0.6) suggests weak market confidence. The Cavaliers deal is unconfirmed and its financial impact is unknown.

- **Confirming evidence to watch:** Look for official confirmation of the Cavaliers stake and its terms, including purchase price and expected return. Monitor quarterly fee-related earnings growth and any guidance from management on achieving low-to-mid-teens CAGR. A sustained drop in short interest or a positive revision from UBS or other analysts would be supportive. Also watch for improvement in the 1-month peer lag (currently -20.9%).

- **Bottom line:** OWL is a deeply lagging asset manager with high short interest and a low forward P/E, but the fundamental case for a turn is unproven. The only qualitative catalyst is an unconfirmed minority sports stake, and the leading sell-side analyst sees more bear than bull factors through 2028. Without verified positive earnings momentum or a confirmed catalyst, the long setup lacks sufficient qualitative support.

sources.title: Is Global Payments Inc. (GPN) A Good Stock To Buy Now?
sources.excerpt: **Is GPN a good stock to buy?** We came across a [bullish thesis ](https://mispricingdesk.substack.com/p/global-payments-still-trades-the)on Global Payments Inc. on The Mispricing Desk's Substack. In this article, we will summarize the bulls' thesis on GPN. Global Payments Inc.'s share was trading at $67.71 as of June 12th. GPN's trailing and forward P/E were 24.89 and 4.91 respectively according to Yahoo Finance. DLocal (DLO) Climbs 9.4% as Revenues Break Past $1 Billion Global Payments Inc. provides payment technology and software solutions for card, check, and digital-based payments in the Americas and internationally. GPN is emerging as a compelling capital-return and earnings-recovery story following the completion of its Worldpay-related portfolio restructuring. While the market continues to focus on integration complexity, acquisition- accounting charges, amortization expenses, and the company's leveraged balance sheet, the underlying business is demonstrating resilient operating performance and significant shareholder value creation. **Read More:**[**15 AI Stocks That Are Quietly Making Investors Rich**](https://www.insidermonkey.com/blog/15-ai-stocks-that-are-quietly- making-investors-rich-1714967/) **Read More:** [**Undervalued AI Stock Poised For Massive Gains: 10000% Upside Potential**](https://www.insidermonkey.com/blog/undervalued-ai-stock-poised- for-massive-gains-10000-upside-19/) In the first quarter of 2026, Global Payments reported 5.5% normalized adjusted net revenue growth, adjusted operating margins of 42.4%, and adjusted diluted EPS of $2.96, while reaffirming full-year adjusted EPS guidance of $13.80 to $14.00 and adjusted net revenue growth of 5% to 6%. More importantly, management has aggressively deployed capital into share repurchases, retiri

analysis:
- **External commentary:** Sources indicate a narrative of ~37.6% undervaluation based on a fair value estimate of $107.16 [1], though the methodology behind that figure is not fully detailed. Susquehanna maintains a Positive rating with a $111 price target, while TD Cowen lowered its target to $74 with a Hold rating, citing cautious near-term headwinds [2]. A bullish thesis highlights GPN as a capital-return and earnings-recovery story post-Worldpay restructuring, with Q1 2026 showing 5.5% normalized revenue growth and reaffirmed EPS guidance [3].

- **Why it can work:** The company’s pivot toward merchant services and software, combined with aggressive share repurchases and a low forward P/E of ~4.0, could support a re-rating if operational momentum continues. Underlying revenue growth (5–6% guided) and adjusted operating margins above 42% suggest the core business is resilient despite market skepticism [3]. The RSI acceleration from -3.1 to +16.1 may indicate short-term momentum shift, but this is not a fundamental catalyst.

- **What can break it:** Integration complexity from the Worldpay restructuring and a leveraged balance sheet remain key risks [3]. TD Cowen’s downgrade reflects “previously disclosed headwinds” that may materialize [2]. The stock is down ~26% from its 52-week high and underperforming its sector by ~8.7% in the past month, signaling persistent negative sentiment. Short interest at 5.3% is moderate but could rise if earnings disappoint.

- **Confirming evidence to watch:** Monitor Q2 2026 earnings for revenue growth consistency with the 5–6% guidance and any updates on debt reduction or share buyback pace. Analyst revisions—particularly whether TD Cowen’s cautious view is validated or reversed—will be critical. A sustained move above the $70 level on above-average volume could signal sentiment shift, but this is not guaranteed.

- **Bottom line:** GPN presents a potential value-recovery setup supported by low valuation multiples, active capital returns, and a merchant pivot narrative. However, skepticism is warranted given unresolved integration risks, mixed analyst views, and recent price underperformance. The long thesis depends on execution against guidance and a reduction in perceived balance-sheet risk; neither is confirmed by current data. No trade recommendation.

sources.title: Pzena Focused Value Strategy Added Cognizant Technology Solutions Corporation (CTSH) Ignoring Overblown AI Risk
sources.excerpt: [Pzena Investment Management](https://www.insidermonkey.com/hedge-fund/pzena- investment-management/435/) recently released its first-quarter 2026 commentary for "Pzena Focused Value Strategy." A copy of the letter can be [downloaded here](https://www.insidermonkey.com/blog/pzena-focused-value- strategys-q1-2026-investor-letter-1787460/). The equity market faced challenges in the first quarter due to the Iran conflict and the related energy price hike, compounded by continued AI-related uncertainty. Despite this volatility, energy stocks led the value index higher, but the portfolio fell short of the index and returned -4.7% (net) vs. 2.1% for the Russell 1000 Value Index. Despite the uncertainty, the firm believes that the current environment presents a strong long-term opportunity. In addition, you can check the Fund's top five holdings to see its best picks for 2026. In its first-quarter 2026 investor letter, Pzena Focused Value Strategy highlighted Cognizant Technology Solutions Corporation (NASDAQ:[CTSH](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/CTSH)). Cognizant Technology Solutions Corporation (NASDAQ:CTSH) is a professional services company that offers consulting and technology, and outsourcing services. On June 22, 2026, Cognizant Technology Solutions Corporation (NASDAQ:CTSH) closed at $41.83 per share. One-month return of Cognizant Technology Solutions Corporation (NASDAQ:CTSH) was -19.78%, and its shares lost 46.23% over the past 52 weeks. Cognizant Technology Solutions Corporation (NASDAQ:CTSH) has a market capitalization of $19.79 billion. Pzena Focused Value Strategy stated the following regarding Cognizant Technology Solutions Corporation (NASDAQ:CTSH) in its Q1 2026 investor letter: > "The health care, financials, and technology sectors were the largest > detract

analysis:
- **External commentary**: Pzena Focused Value Strategy added CTSH in Q1 2026, calling the AI risk "overblown" and viewing the current environment as a "strong long-term opportunity" [1]. The fund noted CTSH fell 46.23% over 52 weeks and was a top detractor due to health care, financials, and technology sector weakness [1]. No other external sources were provided; management or analyst commentary beyond this is unknown.

- **Why it can work**: CTSH trades at a forward P/E of 8.59 and a PEG of 0.87, suggesting the market is pricing in low growth expectations. The RSI acceleration from -3.6 to +11.2 and a 12.7% short float indicate potential for a short squeeze if sentiment shifts. Pzena's thesis that AI disruption risk is overblown implies current valuation may already discount worst-case scenarios, leaving room for recovery if earnings stabilize.

- **What can break it**: The stock is down 52.7% from its 52-week high and 21.4% in the past month, with a sentiment score of only 0.2 (very negative). The 1-month peer lag of 15% suggests CTSH is underperforming its sector materially. If AI-driven demand shifts permanently away from legacy IT services, or if macro headwinds (e.g., energy price hikes, Iran conflict) persist, earnings could deteriorate further, making the low P/E a value trap.

- **Confirming evidence to watch**: Look for stabilization in revenue growth or margin guidance in the next earnings report, a reduction in the short float (currently 12.7%), and any positive analyst revisions to forward estimates. Also monitor sector-relative performance: if CTSH stops lagging peers (peer lag >15% is extreme), it may signal a turning point.

- **Bottom line**: CTSH is a deeply out-of-favor value play with a low forward P/E and high short interest, supported by one value-oriented fund's contrarian bet. However, the extreme price decline, negative sentiment, and lack of positive catalysts from provided sources mean the setup is speculative. Without evidence of a fundamental turnaround, the long thesis relies heavily on mean reversion and the "overblown AI risk" narrative, which is unproven.

sources.title: Kroger's Board of Directors Raises Quarterly Dividend by 11%
sources.excerpt: CINCINNATI, June 25, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- The Kroger Co.'s (NYSE: KR) Board of Directors approved a dividend increase from $1.40 to $1.56 per year. The next quarterly dividend of 39 cents per share will be paid on September 1, 2026, to shareholders of record as of close of business on August 15, 2026. The Kroger Co. Logo The company's quarterly dividend has grown at a 13% compounded annual growth rate since it was reinstated in 2006. This marks the 20th consecutive year of dividend increases. The company continues to expect, subject to board approval, an increasing dividend over time. "This dividend increase reflects the Board of Directors' confidence in Kroger's operating performance, durable free cash flow generation, and commitment to deliver long-term value for shareholders," said Ron Sargent, Chairman of Kroger's Board of Directors. Kroger remains committed to balanced capital allocation by investing in the business to drive sustainable growth, maintaining its current investment grade debt rating and returning capital to shareholders. **About Kroger** The [Kroger](https://www.kroger.com/) Co. (NYSE: KR) is one of America's largest retailers, serving more than 11 million customers daily through a digital shopping experience and retail food stores under a variety of [banner names](https://www.thekrogerco.com/about/our-business/). With more than 400,000 associates across our family of companies, Kroger is committed to providing America with affordable, great-tasting food and creating #ZeroHungerZeroWaste communities. To learn more about us, visit our [newsroom](https://ir.kroger.com/news/default.aspx) and [investor relations](https://edge.prnewswire.com/c/link/?t=0&l=en&o=4717663-1&h=313921986&u=https%3A%2F%2Fir.kroger.com%2Foverview%2Fdefault.aspx&a=investor+relation

sources.title: The 5 Most Interesting Analyst Questions From Kroger’s Q1 Earnings Call
sources.excerpt: The 5 Most Interesting Analyst Questions From Kroger's Q1 Earnings Call Kroger's first quarter saw a negative market reaction as the company's profit missed Wall Street's expectations, despite revenue exceeding consensus and ongoing growth in key areas. Management pointed to rising operating costs outpacing sales and highlighted uneven execution across stores as meaningful challenges. CEO Greg Foran described the current pace of cost growth as "not sustainable, and frankly, it's not acceptable," emphasizing efforts to close operational gaps and deliver more consistent performance. The company also noted a shift in customer behavior toward more deliberate, value-driven shopping patterns due to inflation and reduced government benefits. Is now the time to buy KR? [Find out in our full research report (it's free).](https://stockstory.org/us/stocks/nyse/kr?utm_source=earningsCall&utm_medium=yahoo&utm_campaign=earningsCallIntroCTA&utm_article=Clpxb2MOZEQ%3D&utm_ticker=KR) ### Kroger (KR) Q1 CY2026 Highlights: * **Revenue:** $46.12 billion vs analyst estimates of $45.46 billion (2.2% year-on-year growth, 1.4% beat) * **EPS (GAAP):** $1.47 vs analyst expectations of $1.60 (8% miss) * **EPS (GAAP) guidance for the full year** is $5.20 at the midpoint, beating analyst estimates by 1.2% * **Operating Margin:** 3.1%, in line with the same quarter last year * **Locations:** 2,828.5 at quarter end, up from 2,789.5 in the same quarter last year * **Same-Store Sales** rose 1% year on year (3.2% in the same quarter last year) * **Market Capitalization:** $39.28 billion While we enjoy listening to the management's commentary, our favorite part of earnings calls is the analyst questions. Those are unscripted and can often highlight topics that management teams would rather avoid or topic

sources.title: Is KR Stock a Buy Now or a Value Trap for Cautious Investors?
sources.excerpt: **The Kroger Co.** KR looks inexpensive, but cheap is not the same as risk- free. The stock's investment case depends on whether low expectations already reflect the pressures facing the grocery chain. KR offers valuation support, cash returns and balance-sheet flexibility. At the same time, softer consumer demand, pharmacy headwinds and margin pressure keep the bullish case from becoming more forceful. ## Why KR Looks Cheap on Earnings Kroger trades at 10.58X forward 12-month earnings, well below the Zacks sub- industry at 35.69X, the broader sector at 22.42X and the S&P 500 at 21.32X. That discount gives value investors a clear reason to keep KR on the watchlist. Still, the valuation case is not built around a sharp re-rating. The $60 price target reflects 11.21X forward 12-month earnings, pointing to modest upside rather than a call for the market to assign Kroger a much higher multiple. Zacks Investment Research Image Source: Zacks Investment Research ## How Kroger Supports Shareholder Returns Kroger's balance sheet adds credibility to the value argument. Net total debt to adjusted EBITDA stood at 1.75 at quarter-end, below the company's target range of 2.30 to 2.50, giving management room to invest while preserving financial flexibility. Capital returns also remain part of the story. Kroger pays a dividend and has a $2 billion share repurchase authorization approved in December 2025, which it expects to complete by the end of fiscal 2026. Management's long-term model targets sustainable total shareholder return of 8-11%, supported by earnings growth, identical sales growth, margin expansion and cash payout. ## What Is Keeping KR From a Stronger Call The main concern is that Kroger's low multiple reflects real operating pressure. Gross margin was 22.7% in the first

analysis:
- **External commentary**: Kroger’s board raised the dividend 11% (to $1.56/year), marking the 20th consecutive annual increase, citing confidence in operating performance and durable free cash flow [1]. However, Q1 FY2026 earnings missed EPS estimates by 8% ($1.47 actual vs. $1.60 expected), with management calling current cost growth “not sustainable” and noting uneven store execution [2]. The stock trades at 10.58x forward earnings, a deep discount to the sub-industry (35.69x) and sector (22.42x), but analysts caution that low multiples may reflect real operating pressure rather than a bargain [3].

- **Why it can work**: The dividend increase signals board-level confidence in cash flow generation, and the net debt-to-EBITDA ratio of 1.75x (below the 2.30–2.50 target) provides balance-sheet flexibility for reinvestment or further shareholder returns [1][3]. A $2 billion buyback authorization (expected to be completed by end of FY2026) and a long-term total shareholder return target of 8–11% offer a tangible capital-return framework [3]. The RSI acceleration from -0.4 to +14.0 and a forward P/E of 11.21x suggest the stock may be pricing in pessimism that could moderate if execution improves.

- **What can break it**: Rising operating costs are outpacing sales growth, and management has explicitly flagged uneven store execution as a meaningful challenge [2]. Consumer behavior is shifting toward more value-driven, deliberate spending due to inflation and reduced government benefits, which could pressure identical sales growth (already decelerated to 1% from 3.2% a year ago) [2]. Gross margin of 22.7% in Q1 is under pressure, and the trailing P/E of 40.88x (due to depressed earnings) highlights that any further profit miss could amplify valuation risk [3]. The negative sentiment score (-0.4) and 24% drawdown from the 52-week high indicate persistent bearish positioning.

- **Confirming evidence to watch**: Monitor next quarter’s operating margin trajectory and management commentary on cost-control initiatives—specifically whether the “not sustainable” cost growth is being addressed. Same-store sales trends (especially ex-fuel) and gross margin stability will be key to validate whether the low forward multiple is a value opportunity or a value trap. Progress on the $2 billion buyback execution and any further dividend increases would reinforce the capital-return narrative. Unknown: whether the RSI inflection (42.7, +13.5 delta) will translate into sustained price momentum without a fundamental catalyst.

- **Bottom line**: Kroger offers a compelling capital-return story (rising dividend, active buyback, low leverage) and a deeply discounted forward multiple, but the setup is contingent on management reversing cost inflation and uneven store execution. The Q1 earnings miss and cautious consumer backdrop introduce real downside risk. Without clear evidence of margin stabilization or sales acceleration, the low valuation alone does not justify a long position. No trade recommendation.

- **External commentary**: No reliable external commentary source was extracted by the web-search pipeline for this run; avoid treating this as a catalyst-backed setup.
- **Why it can work**: Unknown from external commentary. Only deterministic warehouse factors are available.
- **What can break it**: Unknown from external commentary; fundamental or news-driven risks require manual source review.
- **Confirming evidence to watch**: Fresh earnings commentary, management guidance, analyst notes, or company filings that validate the setup.
- **Bottom line**: Source coverage failed, so this card should be read as quantitatively flagged but qualitatively unverified.

sources.title: TG Therapeutics (TGTX) Soars 7.5%: Is Further Upside Left in the Stock?
sources.excerpt: **TG Therapeutics** TGTX shares ended the last trading session 7.5% higher at $46.62. The jump came on an impressive volume with a higher-than-average number of shares changing hands in the session. This compares to the stock's 2% gain over the past four weeks. TG Therapeutics scored a strong price increase, on investors' optimism driven by the recent announcement of positive topline Phase 1 data for BRIUMVI in patients with myasthenia gravis (MG). The company also initiated the Phase 2 clinical trial evaluating BRIUMVI as a maintenance therapy following induction with efgartigimod in adult patients with MG. This biopharmaceutical company is expected to post quarterly earnings of $0.42 per share in its upcoming report, which represents a year-over-year change of +147.1%. Revenues are expected to be $230.64 million, up 63.4% from the year- ago quarter. Earnings and revenue growth expectations certainly give a good sense of the potential strength in a stock, but empirical research shows that trends in earnings estimate revisions are strongly correlated with near-term stock price movements. For TG Therapeutics, the consensus EPS estimate for the quarter has been revised 10.6% higher over the last 30 days to the current level. And a positive trend in earnings estimate revision usually translates into price appreciation. So, make sure to keep an eye on TGTX going forward to see if this recent jump can turn into more strength down the road. The stock currently carries a Zacks Rank #3 (Hold). You can see the complete list of today's Zacks Rank #1 (Strong Buy) stocks here >>>> TG Therapeutics belongs to the Zacks Medical - Biomedical and Genetics industry. Another stock from the same industry, **Nurix Therapeutics, Inc.** NRIX, closed the last trading session 2.2% lower at $15.

Among recent announcements, the Phase 3 ENHANCE trial showing that a single 600 mg Day 1 infusion is bioequivalent to the current two‑step IV initiation is particularly releva

analysis:
- **External commentary:** [1] highlights a bullish thesis centered on BRIUMVI’s commercial traction, recurring revenue, and expanding adoption among neurologists, while [2] notes positive Phase 1 data for BRIUMVI in myasthenia gravis (MG) and a 10.6% upward revision in consensus EPS estimates over 30 days. [3] emphasizes the potential of an at-home subcutaneous BRIUMVI formulation, with a Phase 3 readout expected in late 2026 or early 2027, and mentions the Phase 3 ENHANCE trial showing bioequivalence for a simplified IV initiation.

- **Why it can work:** BRIUMVI’s growing prescription base and patient persistence (per [1]) provide a revenue foundation, while pipeline expansion into MG and subcutaneous dosing (per [2] and [3]) could broaden the addressable market. The forward P/E of 14.26 and trailing P/E of 14.32 suggest moderate valuation relative to earnings, and the 1-month return of +37.9% (warehouse data) may reflect recent positive catalysts. The short float of 18.4% could amplify upside if positive news triggers a squeeze.

- **What can break it:** The company remains a single-product story (BRIUMVI) per [3], making it vulnerable to competitive pressure, regulatory setbacks, or slower-than-expected adoption. The Phase 3 subcutaneous trial is not expected to read out until late 2026/early 2027, leaving a long gap without major catalysts. The RSI acceleration score of -5.2 (warehouse data) suggests recent momentum is fading, and the stock is only 7% off its 52-week high, limiting near-term upside potential.

- **Confirming evidence to watch:** Monitor quarterly earnings and revenue growth (expected +63.4% YoY per [2]) for actual vs. consensus beats. Track prescription trends for BRIUMVI and any updates on the MG Phase 2 trial initiation. Watch for regulatory or payer decisions on the subcutaneous formulation. The warehouse data shows a sentiment score of 1.0 (maximum), which may already be priced in; any deterioration in sentiment could be a warning.

- **Bottom line:** The long setup relies on BRIUMVI’s commercial momentum and pipeline expansion, but the single-product risk and long timeline to the next major catalyst (subcutaneous Phase 3 readout) create uncertainty. The stock’s recent run-up and high sentiment suggest limited margin of safety. No trade recommendation is made.

sources.title: Commercial Metals (CMC) Q3 Earnings: Taking a Look at Key Metrics Versus Estimates
sources.excerpt: For the quarter ended May 2026, Commercial Metals (CMC) reported revenue of $2.48 billion, up 22.9% over the same period last year. EPS came in at $1.73, compared to $0.74 in the year-ago quarter. The reported revenue represents a surprise of +4.88% over the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $2.37 billion. With the consensus EPS estimate being $1.60, the EPS surprise was +8.13%. While investors scrutinize revenue and earnings changes year-over-year and how they compare with Wall Street expectations to determine their next move, some key metrics always offer a more accurate picture of a company's financial health. As these metrics influence top- and bottom-line performance, comparing them to the year-ago numbers and what analysts estimated helps investors project a stock's price performance more accurately. sources.title: Commercial Metals (CMC) Q3 Earnings and Revenues Beat Estimates
sources.excerpt: Commercial Metals (CMC) came out with quarterly earnings of $1.73 per share, beating the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $1.6 per share. This compares to earnings of $0.74 per share a year ago. These figures are adjusted for non- recurring items. This quarterly report represents an earnings surprise of +8.13%. A quarter ago, it was expected that this manufacturer and recycler of steel and metal products would post earnings of $1.28 per share when it actually produced earnings of $1.16, delivering a surprise of -9.38%. Over the last four quarters, the company has surpassed consensus EPS estimates three times. Commercial Metals, which belongs to the Zacks Steel - Producers industry, posted revenues of $2.48 billion for the quarter ended May 2026, surpassing the Zacks Consensus Estimate by 4.88%. This compares to year-ago revenues of $2.02 billion. The company has topped consensus revenue estimates four times over the last four quarters. The sustainability of the stock's immediate price movement based on the recently-released numbers and future earnings expectations will mostly depend on management's commentary on the earnings call. Commercial Metals shares have added about 3% since the beginning of the year versus the S&P 500's gain of 7.5%. ## What's Next for Commercial Metals? While Commercial Metals has underperformed the market so far this year, the question that comes to investors' minds is: what's next for the stock? There are no easy answers to this key question, but one reliable measure that can help investors address this is the company's earnings outlook. Not only does this include current consensus earnings expectations for the coming quarter(s), but also how these expectations have changed lately. sources.title: CMC Reports Third Quarter of Fiscal 2026 Results
sources.excerpt: Relative to the fiscal second quarter, significant improvement from the Construction Solutions Group ("CSG") and Europe Steel Group, more than offset headwinds in the North America Steel Group adjusted EBITDA. Core EBITDA margins expanded 20 basis points sequentially.1 **Business Segments - Fiscal Third Quarter 2026 Review** **North America Steel Group** Third quarter North America Steel Group adjusted EBITDA was $253.5 million, an increase of 41% year-over-year driven by higher margins over scrap costs and benefits from CMC's TAG program. Metal margins increased $111 per ton, with average selling price for steel products increasing $130 per ton, while scrap costs were up only $19 per ton over the same timeframe. As a result, adjusted EBITDA margin was 14.2%, up from 11.5% in the prior year period. Finished goods shipment volumes for the North America Steel Group decreased 1.7% versus the prior year due to temporary inventory constraints related to the planned downtime, several lost shipping days due to heavy rainfall, which curtailed construction activity in certain markets, and increased commercial discipline in focusing on value over volume. Despite these headwinds in the third quarter, underlying demand remains solid. The project pipeline continues to grow, supported by public infrastructure spending, as well as mega-projects investments across data centers, semiconductors, and ongoing energy-related build outs, all of which are contributing to a healthy backlog. Downstream backlog volumes remained elevated above historical averages, with third quarter booking pricing increasing 15.5% versus the prior year period. On a sequential basis, segment profitability moderated due to a combination of three factors. First, planned maintenance outages across a number of mill o

analysis:
- **External commentary**: Q3 FY2026 results show revenue of $2.48B (+22.9% YoY) and EPS of $1.73 (+133.8% YoY), beating consensus estimates by 4.88% and 8.13% respectively [1][2]. North America Steel Group adjusted EBITDA rose 41% YoY to $253.5M, driven by higher metal margins (+$111/ton) and the TAG program; downstream booking pricing increased 15.5% YoY [3]. Europe Steel Group and Construction Solutions Group improved sequentially [3]. Shares have underperformed the S&P 500 YTD (+3% vs. +7.5%) [2].

- **Why it can work**: Strong earnings momentum with three of the last four quarters beating EPS estimates [2]. Underlying demand is described as "solid" with a growing project pipeline from public infrastructure, data centers, semiconductors, and energy-related builds [3]. Downstream backlog volumes remain "elevated above historical averages," suggesting future revenue visibility [3]. The forward P/E of 10.66x is below the trailing P/E of 16.45x, implying expected earnings growth, though the PEG ratio of 13.13x is high. The RSI at 39.7 is below 40, potentially indicating a pullback in an uptrend, with RSI acceleration of +3.8 from a prior negative average.

- **What can break it**: North America Steel Group finished goods shipment volumes fell 1.7% YoY due to planned downtime, weather-related construction delays, and a focus on value over volume [3]. Temporary inventory constraints and lost shipping days may recur. The Europe Steel Group and CSG improvements are sequential, not necessarily sustainable. The stock has underperformed the broader market YTD [2]. High PEG ratio (13.13x) suggests current valuation may not reflect long-term growth. Short interest at 4.6% of float indicates some bearish positioning. The prior quarter missed EPS estimates by -9.38% [2], showing earnings can disappoint.

- **Confirming evidence to watch**: Monitor next quarter's shipment volumes for recovery from the 1.7% YoY decline. Track downstream backlog conversion rates and booking pricing trends. Watch for continued sequential improvement in Europe Steel Group and CSG adjusted EBITDA. Verify if metal margins over scrap costs can sustain or expand. Check management commentary on demand from infrastructure and mega-projects. Observe if RSI continues to recover above 40 and if price holds above recent lows.

- **Bottom line**: The company has delivered strong earnings beats and revenue growth, with a solid demand narrative from infrastructure and mega-projects. However, shipment volumes declined, and the stock has lagged the market. The setup appears to be a momentum pullback play based on recent RSI acceleration from oversold levels, but the fundamental drivers are mixed—strong margins versus volume headwinds. The high PEG ratio and prior earnings miss caution against assuming sustained growth. No trade recommendation.

sources.title: Is Galaxy Digital (GLXY) A Good Stock To Buy Now?
sources.excerpt: **Is GLXY a good stock to buy?** We came across a [bullish thesis ](https://traderspro.substack.com/p/galaxy-digital-glxy-two-megatrends)on Galaxy Digital on TradersPro's Substack. In this article, we will summarize the bulls' thesis on GLXY. Galaxy Digital's share was trading at $33.36 as of June 12th. GLXY's trailing and forward P/E were 47.25 and 666.67 respectively according to Yahoo Finance. Galaxy Digital Inc. (GLXY) is positioned as a differentiated infrastructure and financial services platform at the intersection of two of the most powerful global megatrends: artificial intelligence and digital assets. The company operates through two core segments, with its digital assets platform serving institutional clients across trading, lending, custody, and crypto- asset management, while its infrastructure arm develops and operates large- scale data centers in West Texas designed to support AI and high-performance computing workloads. **Read More:**[**15 AI Stocks That Are Quietly Making Investors Rich**](https://www.insidermonkey.com/blog/15-ai-stocks-that-are-quietly- making-investors-rich-1714967/) **Read More:** [**Undervalued AI Stock Poised For Massive Gains: 10000% Upside Potential**](https://www.insidermonkey.com/blog/undervalued-ai-stock-poised- for-massive-gains-10000-upside-19/) This dual structure allows Galaxy to capture demand from both rising institutional crypto adoption and accelerating AI compute requirements, creating a rare two-engine growth model within a single platform. The investment case is increasingly supported by structural constraints in global AI expansion, where electricity availability has become the primary bottleneck rather than chip supply alone. Galaxy's strategically located and power-rich Texas data center footprint represents a sc

analysis:
- **External commentary:** The cited bullish thesis [1] frames Galaxy Digital as a dual‑engine platform capturing both institutional crypto adoption and AI compute demand, with its Texas data centers positioned to benefit from electricity‑supply constraints that are now the primary bottleneck for AI expansion. The source notes a forward P/E of 666.67 as of June 12, implying extreme earnings expectations. No source excerpts discuss management quality, balance sheet risks, or regulatory exposure.

- **Why it can work:** The structural thesis [1] suggests Galaxy’s infrastructure segment could monetize the AI‑driven need for power‑rich data centers, while its digital‑assets platform may gain from rising institutional crypto activity. If both megatrends accelerate simultaneously, the company could see revenue growth from two uncorrelated drivers. The current RSI of 42.4 and 1‑month return of +2.5% (lagging the sector median of +5.9%) may indicate a pullback that could re‑rate if catalysts emerge.

- **What can break it:** The forward P/E of 326.07 implies the market already prices in substantial future growth; any disappointment in AI data‑center utilization or crypto trading volumes could trigger a sharp de‑rating. The stock is 33.9% below its 52‑week high, suggesting prior optimism faded. Unknown: Galaxy’s actual data‑center power contracts, utilization rates, and the sustainability of its crypto revenue streams. Regulatory or competitive shifts in either AI infrastructure or digital assets are not addressed in the excerpts.

- **Confirming evidence to watch:** Look for quarterly updates on data‑center occupancy, power‑purchase agreements, and AI client additions. Also monitor Galaxy’s digital‑assets trading and lending volumes, as well as Bitcoin/Ether price trends, since crypto exposure is a key driver. Any insider buying or analyst upgrades would be incremental, but no such data is provided.

- **Bottom line:** The long setup rests on a plausible but unverified dual‑megatrend narrative [1] at a very high valuation. The warehouse metrics show a pullback in a lagging stock (RSI 42.4, 1‑month return below sector median), but the opportunity score of 79.7 is not supported by fundamental data in the excerpts. Without evidence of execution or margin trends, the thesis remains speculative. No trade recommendation.

sources.excerpt: Bio-Techne (TECH) has an average rating of overweight and mean price target of $65.33, according to Upgrade to read this MT Newswires article and get so much more. A Silver or Gold subscription plan is required to access premium news articles.

analysis:
- **External commentary:** [2] reports that Bio-Techne is to be acquired by Merck for $11.3 billion, which is a definitive M&A catalyst. [3] notes a Leerink downgrade to Market Perform with a $73 price target, and an average analyst rating of overweight with a mean target of $65.33. [1] provides only general market context (mixed indexes, inflation data) with no specific Bio-Techne analysis.

- **Why it can work:** The announced acquisition by Merck at $11.3 billion provides a clear, premium-based upside catalyst. The current price of $70.545 is above the mean analyst target of $65.33 but below the Leerink target of $73, suggesting potential for further price convergence toward the deal value. The RSI acceleration (8.8) and 1-month return (+22.1%) indicate strong momentum, possibly reflecting market anticipation of the deal closing.

- **What can break it:** The acquisition is subject to regulatory approvals, antitrust review, and shareholder votes—any delay or blockage would remove the primary catalyst. The Leerink downgrade to Market Perform signals that some analysts see limited upside from current levels. The trailing P/E of 75.21 is extremely high, and the forward P/E of 25.54 still implies elevated expectations if the deal fails. Short interest at 8.8% suggests some skepticism.

- **Confirming evidence to watch:** Regulatory filings (e.g., Hart-Scott-Rodino clearance, EU/CFIUS approvals), Merck’s financing details, and any shareholder vote dates. Monitor the spread between TECH’s stock price and the implied deal value ($11.3B / shares outstanding) to gauge market confidence in closing. Also watch for any competing bids or activist involvement.

- **Bottom line:** The long setup is entirely dependent on the Merck acquisition closing. Without the deal, the stock’s high valuation (trailing P/E 75x) and analyst downgrade suggest limited fundamental support. The RSI breakout and momentum are likely deal-driven, not organic. No trade recommendation—this is a binary event with significant regulatory and execution risk.

sources.title: 2 Reasons to Like TRU (and 1 Not So Much)
sources.excerpt: 2 Reasons to Like TRU (and 1 Not So Much) TransUnion's stock price has taken a beating over the past six months, shedding 22.2% of its value and falling to $68 per share. This may have investors wondering how to approach the situation. Following the drawdown, is now an opportune time to buy TRU? [Find out in our full research report, it's free](https://stockstory.org/us/stocks/nyse/tru?utm_source=threeReasons&utm_medium=yahoo&utm_campaign=introResearchReport&utm_article=ClpxbmsHZ0A%3D&utm_ticker=TRU). ## Why Does TRU Stock Spark Debate? One of the three major credit bureaus in the United States alongside Equifax and Experian, TransUnion (NYSE:TRU) is a global information and insights company that provides credit reports, fraud prevention tools, and data analytics to help businesses make decisions and consumers manage their financial health. ## Two Things to Like: ### 1\. Skyrocketing Revenue Shows Strong Momentum Examining a company's long-term performance can provide clues about its quality. Any business can have short-term success, but a top-tier one grows for years. Thankfully, TransUnion's 11.6% annualized revenue growth over the last five years was excellent. Its growth beat the average business services company and shows its offerings resonate with customers. TransUnion Quarterly Revenue ### 2\. Projected Revenue Growth Is Remarkable Forecasted revenues by Wall Street analysts signal a company's potential. Predictions may not always be accurate, but accelerating growth typically boosts valuation multiples and stock prices while slowing growth does the opposite, though some deceleration is natural as businesses become larger. Over the next 12 months, sell-side analysts expect TransUnion's revenue to rise by 11.3%, close to its 11.6% annualized growth for the past f

analysis:
- **External commentary:** [1] highlights TransUnion’s strong five-year revenue CAGR of 11.6% and sell-side expectations for 11.3% revenue growth over the next 12 months, calling the recent 22% price decline a potential opportunity. The same source notes one unspecified negative (“1 Not So Much”) but does not detail it in the excerpt. No other external sources are provided to corroborate or challenge this view.

- **Why it can work:** The company’s revenue growth rate (11.6% historical, ~11.3% projected) is above average for business services, and the forward P/E of 12.33 is below the trailing P/E of 19.06, implying earnings expansion is expected. The RSI acceleration (5.4) and short interest (5.1% of float) suggest some bearish positioning that could unwind if sentiment shifts. The composite value score (52.3) is neutral, but the opportunity score (78.4) and squeeze-laggard score (78.4) indicate the stock is relatively cheap vs. peers and may be set for a mean-reversion move.

- **What can break it:** The stock is down 31.6% from its 52-week high and underperformed its sector by 9.6% in the last month. The single negative factor in [1] is unknown, but could relate to debt, regulatory risk, or competitive pressure from Equifax/Experian. The PEG ratio of 1.13 suggests growth is already priced in, leaving little room for error. If projected revenue growth disappoints or the broader financial sector weakens, the stock could continue to lag.

- **Confirming evidence to watch:** Look for upcoming quarterly revenue and earnings reports to confirm the 11.3% growth forecast. Monitor changes in short interest (currently 5.1%) and RSI momentum (currently 55.6, accelerating). A sustained move above the 52-week low (+7.3%) on above-average volume would suggest buying interest. Any positive analyst revisions or debt reduction announcements would support the thesis.

- **Bottom line:** TransUnion shows a compelling revenue growth narrative and a relatively low forward multiple, but the stock is a laggard in a strong sector and the single negative factor from [1] is unknown. The setup is speculative and depends on execution against growth expectations. No trade recommendation is made.
